⚠Your changes could not be saved on this device.
You are offline — transactions are being saved locally and will sync when you reconnect.
SMS Backup
No internet? Copy this text and send it as an SMS to your own number — you can re-enter it later when you are back online.
All transactions are also saved on this device and will sync automatically when you reconnect.

First steps guide
Made for South African farmers
Works offline · No accountant needed
Your data stays on your device
AgriInsights
Choose your language
Kies jou taal
Select the language you want to use throughout the app.
Kies die taal wat jy in die program wil gebruik.
🇬🇧
English
Use the app in English
🇿🇦
Afrikaans
Gebruik die program in Afrikaans
Step 2 of 5
Tell us about your farm
This appears on your reports and statements. You can change it anytime in Settings.
Sets how we estimate your provisional tax. Not sure? Leave as Sole proprietor — change it anytime in Settings.
Why we ask. If you’re VAT-registered, we split the 15% out of your sales and costs and track what you owe or reclaim on your VAT201. If not, we leave your amounts exactly as entered. Most smaller farmers only register once turnover passes R1 million.
No — not registeredTurnover under R1m, or not yet registered.
🧾
Yes — I charge 15% VATSARS-registered; you file a VAT201.
✅
Your category is how often SARS wants your VAT201 — it’s on your VAT registration letter or on SARS eFiling. A / B file every 2 months (SARS picks which). D is the 6-month farming cycle. C is monthly, E once a year. Not sure? Leave it on 2-monthly and confirm with your accountant.
Step 3 of 5
What do you mainly farm?
Pick the one that fits. Running more than one? Choose Mixed Farm and tick exactly what you run.
🌾
Grain & Field Crops
Maize, wheat, soya, sunflower
🍊
Orchards, Fruit & Veg
Citrus, apples, berries, veg
🐄
Livestock
Cattle, sheep, goats, dairy
🌿
Mixed Farm
A combination — most common
Which do you run? Tick all that apply
🌾

Step 4 of 5
How do you want to start?
Choose how you'd like to use AgriInsights today.
🔍 Show me around with demo data
Load a sample farm so you can explore all the features before adding your own numbers. Demo data can be cleared anytime.
Recommended for new users
✍️ Start with my own data
Clear the demo data and start recording your real farm income and expenses from today. You'll be guided on what to add first.
📥Do you have last season's records to bring in?
Yes — I'll bring them inImport a bank statement or co-op file next.
📄
No — I'll add as I goStart fresh; bring in history anytime later.
✏️
🎉
Your farm is ready!
Here's what to do first to get the most out of AgriInsights.
✅
Overview complete!
You’ve seen the whole app. Every tab also has its own detailed walkthrough — handy when you want to go deeper on Crops, Money Flow or Compliance.
👆 Look for “▶ Demo this page” at the top of any screen to start that tab’s walkthrough.

AgriInsights · May 2024
Am I on track?
Not set up yet
Let’s set your goals for the year
Two numbers — what you expect to earn and what you expect to spend — and every week this page will tell you if you’re on track. Pick the easiest way to start:
💡 Whatever you pick, nothing is locked — you can fine-tune any month afterwards, and this page only starts judging once your targets are in.

Insights
Asset Register
Asset Category Purchased Purchase Price Tax value Write-off this yr
No assets added yet. Click + Add Asset to start your register.
Section 12B — Agricultural Asset Depreciation
Farm equipment, irrigation and storage qualify for accelerated depreciation: 50% year 1, 30% year 2, 20% year 3. This reduces your taxable income and flows directly into your Tax Summary report.
Add Asset
✓ detected
We've set the usual period — your accountant can fine-tune it.
Net value you ownR0
Invoice, registration, title deed, warranty or a photo.
Asset
Lender Pack — Asset Schedule
For a finance / loan application
Let's check your figures
A bank report is only as strong as what's behind it. Add what we're missing, and check anything we've estimated.
Bankability Report
A lender-ready summary of your farm's finances, built from your records. First — what do you need it for?
Pick up where you left off
or
Either way, every figure comes from your records or is confirmed by you before anything is shared — no made-up numbers reach the bank.
Model it
Your income
Afford it?
Report

Can you afford a loan?

SA rates · prime %
Model any loan and see straight away whether the farm — and the family — can carry it, before you go to the bank.
I know the amount
Work out what I can afford
R
This sets a starting rate — better security means a smaller margin over prime.
🌍 Land 🚜 Machinery 💧 Irrigation 🏚 Buildings 🌾 Season inputs 🐄 Livestock
%

Let's use your real numbers

We start from your records — but you own every forward figure, so nothing is assumed. Change anything that doesn't look right.
Your net farm income, per season
This year comes from your records. Pick what you expect next — or type each season. Every figure is yours to change.
Same each year A bit higher A bit lower
This year records
R
Year 2
R
Year 3
R
Income after running costs, before tax and loan repayments. We check the loan against your leanest of these three seasons.
Will this loan bring in extra income?
R
What the farm has to cover first
You repay a loan from what's left after tax and what your family lives on. This is the honest test.
R
Food, school fees, the home bond — what you draw out of the farm to live on.
R
A partner's salary, rent you receive, a pension — steady money from off the farm. Leave blank if none.
recordsIncome tax is pulled from your Tax & SARS figures — we take it off automatically, so you don't have to.

Can you afford it?

Two simple checks: will the bank say yes, and will there be money left over for you?
The bank's check
A figure like 1.30× just means the farm earns R1.30 for every R1 the loans cost that year. Banks like to see at least 1.25× (R1.25 for every R1).
What's actually left for youleanest year
The most you can safely borrow
—
The biggest loan you could take at this term and rate and still earn at least R1.25 for every R1 of repayments — measured on your leanest season, before any new income helps.
Estimates to help you plan, using the current SA prime rate and the income-to-repayments level SA agri lenders (Land Bank, ABSA, Standard Bank, Nedbank, FNB) typically look for. Tax, existing loans and income come from your records; living costs, outside income and forward seasons are yours. Your bank prices to your own risk and security. Not a credit decision or an offer to lend.

Take it to your bank

Everything you just entered — plus your records — becomes a lender-ready Bankability Report. Nothing is typed twice.
Bankability Report
A one-pack credit summary a loan officer can act on, assembled from what you already have.
  • Your loan request & pro-forma coveryou entered
  • Debt serviceability & stress testfrom records
  • Existing debt, cash-flow & collateralfrom records
  • Track record, balance sheet & SARS standingfrom records
You'll preview every figure and its source before anything downloads or is shared — you stay in control of what the bank sees.
Your Profit Margins
What each part of your farm really earns — and what it costs to produce.
Workers
Your workers, their pay & staying on the right side of the law
Overview
Workers
Payroll
Compliance
Add a worker
Workers · My Farm
Who are they?
Most workers do a bit of everything — just describe their main job.
Lives on the farm?
Turns on the housing & tenure rules (ESTA).
What you pay them
The law’s normal limit is 45 hours a week — change it to match how this worker actually works.
R
⌛Enter a wage and we’ll check it against the minimum.
Sign them up for UIF
1% off them, 1% from you — worked out each payday.
The paperwork
Stored on the worker’s file. Skip it and we’ll flag the contract as still to do.
We’ll open the contract builder right after you add them. The law needs signed particulars on file.
Money in between — optional
They owe you an advance
Money you’ve fronted them — paid back off their wage.
R
R
Counts down on its own until it’s cleared. You can add another advance anytime — like when there’s a funeral.
Funeral & savings
A bit put aside each payday — for a funeral, or just savings.
Where does the money go?
How often?
R
No fixed amount — you add money when you can, and we keep the running total.
I have their written okay
The law needs written consent for any deduction — we keep it on file.
Loans & Debt
What you owe, what it really costs you, and when you’ll be done paying.
Tax & SARS
What to set aside, what’s due and when — in plain words.
Livestock
Overview of your herd
Herd
Breeding
Health
Plan
Record a movement
Move animals to another camp

⚙ Targets the insights compare against

Breeding rates come from the Land Bank Enterprise Budget — change any to match your operation. Off-take has no industry benchmark; set your own goal if you want a status on it.

Plan a new line

A what-if for stock you don’t run yet. Nothing is added to your herd until you save the plan.
Each entry does two jobs: the Cost feeds your SARS S11(a) deductions and the Status tracks the safe-to-sell date — log a vaccination once, not twice. Births, deaths and sales are recorded under Herd → Records.

A carton is the standard box your fruit is packed and sold in. Citrus is usually 15 kg, apples 12.5 kg, avocados 4 kg — but your packhouse may pack a different size, so set what you actually use.

🌻 SunflowersAgsun 8251 / PAN 7057R1 800–R2 200/100,000 seed bagPlant density ~25,000/ha
🌿 CanolaHyola 555TT / 43C80R4 200–R5 400/1.5kg bag (~1ha)3–5 kg/ha planting rate
🌾 SorghumACNV 9060R2 100–R2 500/50,000 seed bagPlant density ~80,000/ha
Diesel is one of the biggest costs on most farms — bulk storage and the SARS diesel rebate both help take the sting out.

Recurring items in the forecast
+R0/mo −R0/mo
These items are added on top of your crop plan in the forecast. To edit, head to settings › recurring.
How the forecast is calculated: your cash on hand now + everything you've captured that moves money — crop plan, livestock, orchards, loan repayments, financed assets, wages, provisional tax & VAT, and recurring bills.

Bring in your records

Link each to the crop or herd it belongs to, so your enterprise breakdown is exact. We’ve suggested where we can — just check them.

Which bank is this statement from?
We'll auto-detect columns based on each bank's CSV layout. Pick "Other CSV" for anything we don't recognise.

We'll detect columns
Upload your CSV, PDF or photo
Export a CSV, download a PDF, or snap a clear photo of your statement, then drop it here. We read it right on your device — your data never leaves your phone.
or paste statement content
Review and import
We've filled in each transaction for you. Tap any field to change it — and tap the green + or red − beside an amount to switch a row between money in and money out. Uncheck anything you don't want to import.
Costs are auto-tagged to a crop or herd where we're confident — check the Enterprise column and change any that are wrong. Tick rows to change several at once.

Paste your co-op settlement statement
Most co-ops email you a PDF + CSV monthly. Copy-paste the delivery lines below — or upload the CSV. We'll match the format.
